summary: Monster Dark Star/Attics sandwich and ferocious Alligator‑Other One jam
blurb: The early acoustic set flows into a high‑energy electric half highlighted by an epic Dark Star → Attics Of My Life sequence and a blistering Alligator > The Other One stretch. Jerry’s lead work is on fire, Phil lays down deep bass foundations, and the dual drummers drive relentless momentum.
review: June 24, 1970 at the Capitol Theatre captured one of the Dead’s most exhilarating early‑summer outings. After a solid acoustic opening – Cold Rain And Snow, Me And My Uncle and Easy Wind – the band launched into an electric marathon that still feels fresh after more than five decades. The night’s centerpiece is the legendary Dark Star → Attics Of My Life sandwich, described by listeners as “smoking” and “out of this world,” with Garcia’s lead soaring over Lesh’s harmonically rich bass lines. That momentum carries straight into a ferocious Alligator > The Other One jam that showcases the band at peak improvisational intensity. Throughout, Bill Kreutzmann’s manic tom‑tom intro to Not Fade Away (captured on audience tapes) and Mickey Hart’s color‑adding percussion keep the flow tight, while Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocals anchor the set, especially on Me And My Uncle and Good Lovin’. Though the recordings are all audience sources, the best tape (gd_nrps70-06-24.aud.pcrp5.23062.sbeok.flacf) offers surprisingly clear crowd ambience and captures the band‑audience interplay that makes this show legendary.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings uniformly praise the Dark Star → Attics sequence, Alligator > The Other One jam, and overall band chemistry. Positive adjectives such as "exceptional," "soaring," "smoking," and "ferocious" appear across five of six analyses, while only one recording offers a negative comment about St. Stephen. The consensus is strongly positive, justifying a +1.4 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Provides the clearest audience capture of the Dark Star/Attics suite and crowd interaction, with good overall fidelity compared to other tapes.

key_highlights: Monster Dark Star → Attics Of My Life sandwich that defines the night’s momentum.
Ferocious Alligator > The Other One jam delivering peak improvisational intensity.
Tight acoustic opening leading seamlessly into a high‑energy electric set with New Riders interlude adding variety.
Powerful dual‑drummer drive and Jerry’s smoking lead throughout.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as "smoking" and "out of this world," delivering soaring, psychedelic‑blues leads especially during the Dark Star and jam sections.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides deep, harmonic bass foundations that lock in with Jerry’s leads, noted for depth during the Dark Star/Attics passages.
band_performance.Bob: Weir supplies solid rhythm guitar and strong vocal work, highlighted on Me And My Uncle and Good Lovin', keeping the set grounded.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart adds tasteful percussive color in later jams, enriching the band’s dynamic texture.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann drives the performance with powerful tom‑tom accents and relentless energy throughout the electric half.
